title: omphalea triandra
intro: Omphalea triandra, also known as the Malabar tree, is a tropical plant species native to India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Omphalea triandra include its potential to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. Omphalea triandra can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or topical paste, and is also used as a component in various herbal remedies and Ayurvedic preparations.
